report_name: EU & UK Sanction Russian GRU, FSB Officers in Joint Cyber Action

threat_actors.name: GRU Unit 29155
threat_actors.description: A unit of Russia's GRU known for covert operations, sabotage, and assassinations. Sanctioned for working with a company named IMPULS to recruit and train hackers from Russian universities. Named officers include Vyacheslav Stafeyev, Ivan Senin, and Ivan Kasyanenko.

threat_actors.name: FSB Centre 16
threat_actors.description: A unit of Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) formally attributed with conducting a reckless cyberattack against Poland's energy grid.

threat_actors.name: Lumma Stealer Developers
threat_actors.description: The developers and operators of the Lumma Stealer malware, sanctioned by the EU and UK for their role in widespread credential theft.

threat_actors.name: Rybar LLC
threat_actors.description: A Russian state-backed media organization sanctioned for spreading anti-Ukraine disinformation and interfering in elections in Moldova and Armenia.

threat_actors.name: Z-Pentest
threat_actors.description: A pro-Russian hacktivist group sanctioned for disruptive attacks on energy and water facilities, acting as a proxy for Russian state interests.

threat_actors.name: Cyber Army of Russia Reborn
threat_actors.description: A pro-Russian hacktivist group sanctioned for disruptive attacks on energy and water facilities, acting as a proxy for Russian state interests.

threat_actors.name: Media Land LLC
threat_actors.description: An entity sanctioned by the EU for its involvement in ransomware and phishing attacks against critical infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as the primary distribution vector for the Lumma Stealer malware.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
attack_patterns.description: A core function of Rybar LLC's disinformation campaigns to gather information on targets.

attack_patterns.name: Keylogging
attack_patterns.description: A common capability of infostealers like Lumma Stealer to harvest credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Develop Capabilities
attack_patterns.description: GRU Unit 29155 worked with the company IMPULS to recruit and train hackers, developing human capabilities for cyber operations.

attack_patterns.name: Loss of Availability
attack_patterns.description: FSB Centre 16 was attributed with an attack on Poland's energy grid that had the potential to cause a massive power outage.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Media Land LLC was sanctioned for involvement in ransomware attacks against critical infrastructure.

malware.name: Lumma Stealer
malware.description: A Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS) infostealer responsible for harvesting credentials from web browsers, cryptocurrency wallets, and other applications. It has affected at least 2,100 UK victims in the last six months.

identities.name: European Union
identities.description: Implemented joint sanctions with the UK against Russian cyber operations.

identities.name: United Kingdom
identities.description: Implemented joint sanctions with the EU against Russian cyber operations. The National Crime Agency reported at least 2,100 UK victims of Lumma Stealer.

identities.name: Poland's energy grid
identities.description: Target of a reckless cyberattack attributed to FSB Centre 16.

identities.name: Government of Moldova
identities.description: Target of election interference by the Russian state-backed media organization Rybar LLC.

identities.name: Government of Armenia
identities.description: Target of election interference by the Russian state-backed media organization Rybar LLC.

identities.name: IMPULS
identities.description: A company that worked with GRU Unit 29155 to recruit and train hackers from Russian universities.

infrastructure.name: Lumma Stealer Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The infrastructure supporting the Lumma Stealer Malware-as-a-Service operation.

infrastructure.name: Russian Disinformation Network
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used by state-backed media organizations like Rybar LLC to spread disinformation and conduct influence operations.

indicators.name: Telegram Domain DNS Query
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Telegram Domain DNS Query
id: 2b8f1c9a-7d3e-4b0f-8c1d-9a3b2c1d0e9f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries for t.me, the domain for the Telegram messaging service, which can be used for C2 or disinformation channels.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
references:
  - https://cybermagazine.com/news/eu-uk-relays-sanctions-against-russia-over-cyber-espionage
level: informational
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 't.me'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Infostealer Access to Chrome Login Data
indicators.pattern:
title: Infostealer Access to Chrome Login Data
id: 5e9a8b7c-6d5f-4e3d-2c1b-0a9f8e7d6c5b
status: experimental
description: Detects processes accessing the Google Chrome 'Login Data' file, a common behavior of infostealer malware like Lumma Stealer.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
references:
  - https://cybermagazine.com/news/eu-uk-relays-sanctions-against-russia-over-cyber-espionage
level: high
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate backup software or security tools.
tags:
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.credential_access
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains|all:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data'
      - '\Login Data'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit

relationships.source_name: Infostealer Access to Chrome Login Data
